decks.description: The legendary photojournalism stock. Characteristics: High contrast, deep blacks, visible 'salt & pepper' grain structure, wide exposure latitude, gritty 'street' aesthetic. Optimized for capturing decisive moments and texture.

prompts_boilerplate.positive: color photography, deep blacks, gritty texture, street photography style, sharp focus, dramatic lighting,
prompts_boilerplate.negative: low contrast, smooth, digital noise, flat lighting, hdr, washed out, blurry, sepia, plastic skin, airbrushed, 3d render, glossy, clean, modern digital look.

sets.name: Hard Noir (Shadow Density)
sets.description: Goal: This is the most critical set. It teaches the LoRA the 'Black Point.' Tri-X is famous for shadows that go deep black but retain just enough detail to not look digital. We need to train the model to render 'zones' of darkness.

sets.name: Human Map (Texture Portraits)
sets.description: Goal: To teach the model that Tri-X is 'sharp but gritty.' We need subjects with distinct skin texture (wrinkles, pores, stubble) to ensure the grain overlay has something to 'bite' into. If we only train on smooth faces, the grain will look like a floating filter.

sets.name: Urban Geometry (Acutance)
sets.description: Goal: 'Acutance' is edge contrast. Tri-X is loved for how it renders concrete, brick, and steel. This set removes organic curves and forces the model to learn sharp, linear transitions. This prevents the LoRA from becoming 'mushy.'

decks.description: The 'Goldilocks' studio stock (1950s-1970s). Characteristics: Fine grain, creamy tonal transitions (satin finish), distinct separation of mid-tones, 'silver' highlights. Less gritty than Tri-X, less clinical than Panatomic-X. The standard for professional portraiture.

prompts_boilerplate.positive: color photography, creamy bokeh, satin finish, studio lighting, smooth gradients, medium contrast, rich mid-tones, professional photography,
prompts_boilerplate.negative: noise, heavy grain, grit, harsh contrast, washed out, digital sharpness, hdr, over-sharpened, plastic skin, 3d render, chromatic aberration, sepia, tinted.

decks.description: The 'Microscope' stock (ISO 32). Characteristics: Virtually invisible grain, infinite resolution, high acutance (edge sharpness), liquid tonality. Captures texture and detail with surgical precision. The antithesis of Tri-X.
